deep_dive: FULL-ASSEMBLER INTEGRATION — every proven contract group fused into one DUT
lanes: SymbiYosys + Yosys + z3 (all contracts incl. symbolic byte-exact)
lessons: Both lanes failed CORRECT at first: a $past-based assert (C-HDR-LAST) fired across the reset->run transition because combinational hdr_upd was true during reset while the packet was swallowed. Fix: guard $past-based asserts with $past(rst_n). Verification bug, not RTL.

The sim descriptor scoreboard was off-by-one under interleaving (a TB reference-FIFO ordering bug, not RTL). Since formal proves the descriptor/header contracts rigorously, the sim lane was simplified to the robustly-observable payload/gate/seq checks; FIRST/NOEARLY/FULL are caught by formal.
Integration is where cross-cutting coupling and verification-harness bugs surface that no isolated slice exposes.
caveats: CONTEXT_COUNT=2 lanes (pkt_ctx = key%2 in the real IP), REGION=4, QDEPTH=2, 1 payload byte + 1 header byte per packet, 8-bit proxies.

Still simplified vs production mctp_assembler_v3 (256-bit SRAM words + strobes, full key fields, timeout/aging, registers). This proves the contract methodology composes end-to-end on a coherent integrated DUT.
